elements.sent-2411-001: The total cross section of this process is measured by ATLAS in proton-proton collisions at a center-of-mass energy $\sqrt{s=13$\,TeV.}
elements.sent-2411-002: The production cross sections for single top-quarks and single top-antiquarks are measured to be $\sigma_{tq =\text{137 ^{+8 _{-8}\,$pb and $\sigma_{\bar{t q =\text{84 ^{+6 _{-5}\,$pb.}}}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-003: For the combined cross section and the ratio of the cross sections $R_t$, $\sigma_{tq+\bar{t q =\text{221 ^{+13 _{-13}\,$pb and $R_t=\text{1.636 ^{+0\text{.. 036 _{-0\text{.}}}}}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-004: As interpretations of the measurement, limits are set on the EFT operator $O_{Qq ^{3,1}$ and on the CKM matrix elements $|V_{td |$, $|V_{ts |$ and $|V_{tb |$.}}}}
elements.sent-2411-005: The main production channel for single top-quarks at the LHC is the $t$-channel exchange of a virtual $W$ boson.
elements.sent-2411-006: With a precise measurement of the process, the Standard Model (SM) prediction can be tested and potential contributions from physics beyond the SM can be probed.
elements.sent-2411-007: The ratio $R_t=\sigma_{tq /\sigma_{\bar{t q}$ provides sensitivity to the predictions of different PDFs and the total cross-section can be used to set constrains on the Effective Field Theory (EFT) operator $O_{Qq ^{3,1}$.}}}
elements.sent-2411-008: These proceedings summarize the results of the latest measurement of the $t$-channel production cross section by ATLAS\cite{atlas using the full Run 2 dataset collected during the years 2015-2018.}
elements.sent-2411-009: All graphics and numbers given in these proceedings are taken from Ref. \cite{2024_tchan .}
elements.sent-2411-010: The events in the signal region are selected according to the expected signature of leading-order $t$-channel events.
elements.sent-2411-011: Exactly one charged light lepton (electron or muon), high missing transverse energy and exactly two jets are required.
elements.sent-2411-012: For the jets, exactly one of them has to be $b$-tagged using the DL1r\cite{DL1r tagger with a 60\% working point.}
elements.sent-2411-013: Additional requirements are imposed to reduce the contribution of background events in the signal region.
elements.sent-2411-014: Two separate signal regions are defined according to the electrical charge of the lepton.
elements.figure-2411-001: \includegraphics[width=0.4\textwidth]{SRn_postFit.pdf} \caption{\label{fig:SRn} $D_\text{nn}$ distribution in the signal region for negative lepton charge after the maximum likelihood fit is performed. The gray band in the lower panel indicates the post-fit uncertainties \cite{2024_tchan}.}
elements.sent-2411-015: To seperate signal and background events, a feed-forward Neural Network (NN) is trained on 17 input variables.
elements.sent-2411-016: A good discriminating power between signal and background events is achieved, as it can be seen from Figure \ref{fig:SRn .}
elements.sent-2411-017: Signal events are classified with high values of the NN output score $D_\text{nn}$, while background events are sorted towards low $D_\text{nn}$ values.
elements.sent-2411-018: The cross sections are determined by performing a binned maximum likelihood fit to the $D_\text{nn}$ distributions.
elements.sent-2411-019: The distribution in Figure \ref{fig:SRn shows the post-fit result.}
elements.sent-2411-020: A good agreement between the MC simulation and the data is reached.
elements.sent-2411-021: The obtained results for the cross sections are $\sigma_{tq =\text{137 ^{+8 _{-8}\,$pb and $\sigma_{\bar{t q =\text{84 ^{+6 _{-5}\,$pb.}}}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-022: The combined cross section and $R_t$ are measured to be $\sigma_{tq+\bar{t q =\text{221 ^{+13 _{-13}\,$pb and $R_t=\text{1.636 ^{+0\text{.. 036 _{-0\text{.}}}}}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-023: A comparison of the result for $R_t$ with the predictions from different PDF sets is presented in Figure \ref{fig:rt .}
elements.sent-2411-024: Most predictions are compatible with the measurement within the uncertainties.
elements.figure-2411-002: \includegraphics[width=0.45\textwidth]{rt.pdf} \caption{\label{fig:rt} Measurement result for $R_t$ compared to the NNLO predictions calculated with MCFM\cite{2021_MCFM} using different PDF sets \cite{2024_tchan}.}
elements.sent-2411-025: As interpretations of the measurement, constrains are set on the EFT operator $O_{Qq ^{3,1}$ and on the CKM matrix elements $|V_{tx |$.}}
elements.sent-2411-026: The EFT operator $O_{Qq ^{3,1}$ introduces a four-fermion contact interaction involving two quarks of the third generation and two quark of the first or second generation.}
elements.sent-2411-027: A non-zero contribution from this operator affects the top-quark production angle and therefore changes the fiducial acceptance $\mathcal{A}$ of the signal events.
elements.sent-2411-028: The signal events $\mathcal{A \times \sigma$ depends quadratically on the Wilson coefficient $C_{Qq ^{3,1 /\Lambda^2$, as shown in \cite{2019_EFT .}}}}
elements.sent-2411-029: This dependence is used for the interpretation.
elements.sent-2411-030: Dedicated MC samples are produced with $C_{Qq ^{3,1 /\Lambda^2$ set to different values.}}
elements.sent-2411-031: From these samples, the expected relative change in the signal event yield is derived as a function of $C_{Qq ^{3,1 /\Lambda^2$.}}
elements.sent-2411-032: The obtained parameterisation is used to perform a binned maximum likelihood fit.
elements.sent-2411-033: Finally, a likelihood scan is performed to extract the 95\% confidence interval on $C_{Qq ^{3,1 /\Lambda^2$.}}
elements.sent-2411-034: The obtained observed confidence interval on $C_{Qq ^{3,1 /\Lambda^2$ is \begin{equation}-0.37 \leq C_{Qq}^{3,1}/\Lambda^2 \leq 0.06,\end{equation} and therefore compatible with the Standard Model.}}
elements.figure-2411-003: \includegraphics[width=0.32\textwidth]{NLLscan_VtbVtd_histo_2D.pdf} \includegraphics[width=0.32\textwidth]{NLLscan_VtbVts_histo_2D.pdf} \includegraphics[width=0.32\textwidth]{NLLscan_VtdVts_histo_2D.pdf} \caption{\label{fig:CKM_results} Confidence contours obtained from the two-dimensional likelihood scans. The difference of the log-likelihood function for points in the two-dimensional plane is taken with regard to the minimum of the function, indicating the best fit result \cite{2024_tchan}.}
elements.sent-2411-035: Both the production and decay of single top quarks involve a $Wtq$ vertex, whose cross-section is proportional to $f_{LV ^2\cdot|V_{tq |^2$.}}
elements.sent-2411-036: The total number of expected signal events is the sum over all combinations of possible production and decay channels \begin{equation}\begin{aligned} N_{\mathrm{sig}} = \displaystyle{\sum_{i=1}^{3}}\displaystyle{\sum_{j=1}^{3}} N_{\mathrm{sig},ij},\ \text{with} N_{\mathrm{sig},ij} = \mathcal{L}\cdot \underbrace{\sigma^{i}_{t} \lvert V_{ti} \rvert^2}_{\mathrm{prod.}} \cdot \underbrace{\mathcal{B}(t\rightarrow jW)}_{\mathrm{decay}}, \text{with}\ \sigma^{i}_{t} = \sigma_{t}(V_{ti}=1),\ \text{flavour indices}\ i,j=b,d,s. \end{aligned}\end{equation} This relation is used to constrain the individual matrix elements $V_{tq}$.
elements.formula-2411-002: \begin{equation} N_{sig} = \displaystyle{\sum_{i=1}^{3}}\displaystyle{\sum_{j=1}^{3}} N_{sig,ij},\ with N_{sig,ij} = L\cdot \underbrace{\sigma^{i}_{t} \lvert V_{ti} \rvert^2}_{prod.} \cdot \underbrace{B(t\rightarrow jW)}_{decay}, with\ \sigma^{i}_{t} = \sigma_{t}(V_{ti}=1),\ flavour indices\ i,j=b,d,s. \end{equation}
elements.sent-2411-037: Dedicated MC samples are produced for the single-top $t$-channel and $t\bar{t}$ processes, modeling the events for all combinations of $Wtq$ vertices in the top-quark production and decay.
elements.sent-2411-038: The limits on the individual matrix elements are set via two-dimensional likelihood scans.
elements.sent-2411-039: For each scan, one parameter is fixed to a certain value, which is zero for $f_{LV}\cdot|V_{td |$, $f_{LV}\cdot|V_{ts |$ and one for $f_{LV}\cdot|V_{tb |$.}}}
elements.sent-2411-040: The results of the scans are presented in Figure \ref{fig:CKM_results .}
elements.sent-2411-041: The production cross-section of single top-quarks produced via the $t$-channel process is measured at the LHC by ATLAS in proton-proton collisions at a center-of-mass energy $\sqrt{s =13$\,TeV.}
elements.sent-2411-042: The cross sections for single top-quark and single top-antiquark production are measured to be $\sigma_{tq =\text{137 ^{+8 _{-8}\,$pb and $\sigma_{\bar{t q =\text{84 ^{+6 _{-5}\,$pb.}}}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-043: For the combined cross section and the ratio $R_t$, $\sigma_{tq+\bar{t q =\text{221 ^{+13 _{-13}\,$pb and $R_t=\text{1.636 ^{+0\text{.. 036 _{-0\text{.}}}}}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-044: This measurement provides the most precise results of the process under study to this date.
elements.sent-2411-045: The theory predictions calculated at NNLO are in good agreement with the results.
elements.sent-2411-046: As interpretations of the measurement, the impact of the EFT operator $O_{Qq ^{3,1}$ is constrained by setting limits on the Wilson coefficient $C_{Qq ^{3,1 /\Lambda^2$.}}}
elements.sent-2411-047: A 95\% confidence interval $-0.37 \leq C_{Qq ^{3,1 /\Lambda^2 \leq 0.06$ is obtained.}}
elements.sent-2411-048: The parameters $f_{LV}\cdot|V_{td |$, $f_{LV}\cdot|V_{ts |$ and $f_{LV}\cdot|V_{tb |$ are constrained via two-dimensional likelihood-scans.}}}
elements.sent-2411-049: All interpretations yield results compatible with the Standard Model.
